title: Flowcharts & Pseudocode
description: How to represent algorithms using flowchart symbols and pseudocode, and how to trace through a flowchart.

steps.title: Do Now Activity
steps.content:
Do Now

Answer these five quick questions from the algorithms lesson.
steps.js: createMCQSet('fc-dna',[{q:'What is an algorithm?',opts:['A set of step-by-step instructions to solve a problem','A type of hardware','A spreadsheet program','A list of files'],correct:0,feedback:'Correct - that is the key definition.',wrongFeedback:'An algorithm is a set of step-by-step instructions to solve a problem.'},{q:'Which word means the steps are clear and not vague?',opts:['Precise','Random','Colourful','Hidden'],correct:0,feedback:'Correct - precise steps are clear and exact.',wrongFeedback:'Precise means clear, exact and not vague.'},{q:'Which step is too vague for a good algorithm?',opts:['Cook it until it looks right','Boil for 10 minutes','Click the green Start button','Write your name in the box'],correct:0,feedback:'Correct - looks right is not precise enough.',wrongFeedback:'A vague instruction does not give enough exact detail.'},{q:'Why should an algorithm be finite?',opts:['So it has a clear end','So it can be drawn in blue','So it never stops','So the first step is ignored'],correct:0,feedback:'Correct - a finite algorithm eventually finishes.',wrongFeedback:'Finite means the algorithm has a clear stopping point.'},{q:'Which pair best describes a good algorithm?',opts:['Ordered and precise','Random and vague','Endless and unclear','Colourful and hidden'],correct:0,feedback:'Correct - good algorithms are ordered and precise.',wrongFeedback:'Good algorithms need clear steps in the correct order.'}]);

steps.title: Two Ways to Show an Algorithm
steps.content:
Two ways to represent an algorithm

Before writing code you can plan the logic in one of two ways:

Flowchart — shapes and arrows

Visual. Each shape has a specific meaning. Easy to see decisions and flow.

Pseudocode — English-like text

Written. Reads like code but is not a real language. Good for planning logic.

START
ASK "What is your name?"
STORE answer in name
OUTPUT "Hello " + name
END

steps.title: Flowchart Symbols
steps.content:
Flowchart symbols

Learn the five shapes — they are always the same in every flowchart.

ShapeNameUsed for
OvalStart / EndBeginning or end of the flowchart
RectangleProcessAn action — e.g. "Add 1 to score"
ParallelogramInput / OutputData in or out — e.g. "Display score"
DiamondDecisionA yes/no question — splits into two paths
ArrowFlow lineShows the direction

Match each shape to its correct purpose:
steps.js:
(function(){var defs=[['Oval','Start or End of the flowchart'],['Rectangle','A process — an instruction or action'],['Diamond','A decision — a yes/no question'],['Parallelogram','An input or output'],['Arrow','Shows the direction of flow']];var opts=defs.map(function(d){return d[1];}).sort(function(){return Math.random()-0.5;});var c=document.getElementById('fc-match');c.innerHTML=defs.map(function(d,i){return '';}).join('')+'
';document.getElementById('fc-check').onclick=function(){var ok=0;defs.forEach(function(d,i){var el=document.getElementById('fc-m-'+i);var pass=el.value===d[1];el.classList.toggle('correct',pass);el.classList.toggle('wrong',!pass);if(pass)ok++;});var fb=document.getElementById('fc-fb');if(ok===defs.length){fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='All correct!';__markStepComplete();}else{fb.className='ex-feedback hint';fb.textContent=ok+' / '+defs.length+'. Review the table and try again.';}};})();

steps.title: Trace a Flowchart
steps.content:
Trace a flowchart

Tracing means following a flowchart step by step. Read this one carefully, then answer the questions.

START
↓
INPUT: number
↓
Decision: Is number ÷ 2 a whole number?
  Yes → OUTPUT: "Even"
  No  → OUTPUT: "Odd"
↓
END
steps.js: createMCQSet('fc-trace',[{q:'The user inputs 6. What does the flowchart output?',opts:['Odd','Even','6','Nothing'],correct:1,feedback:'Correct — 6 ÷ 2 = 3 (whole), so Yes → "Even".',wrongFeedback:'6 ÷ 2 = 3, a whole number. Decision is Yes → output "Even".'},{q:'The user inputs 7. What does the flowchart output?',opts:['Even','7','Odd','Nothing'],correct:2,feedback:'Correct — 7 ÷ 2 = 3.5 (not whole), so No → "Odd".',wrongFeedback:'7 ÷ 2 = 3.5, not a whole number. Decision is No → output "Odd".'},{q:'Which shape is used for the yes/no question in this flowchart?',opts:['Rectangle','Oval','Parallelogram','Diamond'],correct:3,feedback:'Correct — decisions are always shown as a diamond.',wrongFeedback:'Decisions are always diamonds — the two exit paths (yes/no) come out of the two sides.'}]);

quizQuestions.q: What are the two main ways to represent an algorithm?
Which flowchart shape is used for Start or End?
Which flowchart shape is used for a Decision?
Which flowchart shape is used for a Process (an action)?
What shape in a flowchart always has two exit paths (yes and no)?
A flowchart processes the number 4. It asks 'Is number ÷ 2 a whole number?' Which path?
